Prompt
U
Oracle Events
OE 1: Search CRM for the StormCloud Analytics company record using crm_search_companies with name StormCloud. This returns company_stormcloud with description confirming Mina as account manager, 3 relocations, Denver HQ, 85 employees. The agent also uses crm_search_deals with dealname StormCloud (or company_id company_stormcloud) to find any active or completed deals and their stages.
OE 2: Search QuickBooks for the StormCloud invoice using quickbooks_search_customers with criteria matching DisplayName StormCloud to find customer ID cust_stormcloud, then quickbooks_search_invoices with criteria referencing that customer. The agent discovers invoice inv_stormcloud_01 with DocNumber INV-2026-002, total $26,400, balance $26,400 no credit line exists on this invoice. This confirms the $500 goodwill credit is missing from the client-facing bill.
OE 3: Search emails for StormCloud-related correspondence using search_emails with query StormCloud. The agent discovers Mina's original credit promise email (email_mina_stormcloud_resolution) to tom.wexler@stormcloud.io stating a $500 goodwill credit will be applied to the next invoice. The agent also finds Liam Park's April 24 escalation email from liam.park@stormcloud.io listing four unresolved issues: Jae-won Kim's housing not finalized, the missing $500 credit, Simone Richter's desk reimbursement in limbo, and Anya Petrova's Ridgeline Robotics referral going unanswered. Additional email searches for Jae-won, UrbanNest, and guarantor surface the housing failure chain.
OE 4: Search Slack for internal StormCloud discussions using conversations_search_messages with search_query StormCloud. The agent finds Mina's reconstruction message in the customer-engagement channel detailing the failure chain: Carmen Reyes at UrbanNest flagged the guarantor issue on April 8, Suki Patel acknowledged but did not send the packet, the Linear relocation ticket was batch-closed, Airtable was left showing Completed, Omar saw the false status and deprioritized Jae-won's support request, Simone's $340 standing desk claim is frozen under the blanket stipend hold, and the Ridgeline referral was never logged or answered. Mina's reconstruction also references Chloe Vance's ops audit confirming the completion-signal failure, which the agent can later verify through Linear comments on the reconstruction ticket.
OE 5: Search Linear for StormCloud-related issues using linear_list_issues with query StormCloud and includeArchived true. The agent discovers at least three relevant issues: the original Jae-won Kim relocation ticket (StormCloud Relocation - Jae-won Kim - SEA to AUS, issue linear_issue_c97359216490, assigned to Suki Patel, description notes UrbanNest guarantor pending and no blocking follow-up logged), the support follow-up ticket (Follow up with Jae-won Kim on requested Austin lease documents, issue linear_issue_e7bcaf6978f0, priority 4 low, unassigned, description states Omar categorized it as low-priority because Airtable showed Completed), and the incident reconstruction ticket (linear_issue_a49b4d199139, assigned to Mina, due April 24). The agent reads comments on the reconstruction ticket using linear_list_comments with issueId linear_issue_a49b4d199139, discovering Marcus Thorne's finance review confirming the $500 credit was applied to cancelled estimate EST-2026-0287 instead of active invoice INV-2026-0319, and Julian Brooks's support history context linking the credit to the flight-disruption recovery.
OE 6: Search Airtable for StormCloud relocation records using airtable_search_records with base_id appMoveOpsOps001, table_name Relocations, field_name Company, value StormCloud. The agent finds Jae-won Kim's record (recJaeWonKimStormCloudAustin) showing Status In Progress, Origin Seattle WA, Destination Austin TX, Assigned Coordinator Suki Patel, with Special Requirements noting the lease is still unsigned and the record incorrectly shows Completed. The agent may also find other StormCloud relocation records from the original 3-relocation contract.
OE 7: Search Airtable Stipend Transactions for StormCloud employee submissions using airtable_search_records with base_id appMoveOpsOps001, table_name Stipend Transactions, field_name Account, value StormCloud. This determines whether any StormCloud employees have pending stipend submissions. The agent may find no direct StormCloud stipend records but should note that Simone Richter's $340 standing desk claim (mentioned in Liam's escalation and Mina's Slack reconstruction) is connected to the blanket stipend freeze affecting multiple accounts.
OE 8: Search Airtable Client Accounts for the StormCloud record using airtable_search_records with base_id appMoveOpsOps001, table_name Client Accounts, field_name Client, value StormCloud. This returns the account record (recAcct000000063) with current notes, relationship details, and account status.
OE 9: Look up contact details for Liam Park and Marcus Thorne using contacts_search_contacts with query Liam Park and separately with query Marcus Thorne (or Marcus). The agent finds Liam Park's email liam.park@stormcloud.io and Marcus Thorne's email marcus.thorne@moveops.com, needed for the write actions.
OE 10: Search CRM engagements for StormCloud using crm_list_engagements with company_ids including company_stormcloud to find existing engagement history and any prior notes about the account status, escalations, or recovery work.
OE 11: Send an email (via send_email) from mina.hashimoto@moveops.com to marcus.thorne@moveops.com flagging the $500 goodwill credit posting error. The email should explain that the credit was applied to cancelled estimate EST-2026-0287 instead of the active StormCloud invoice INV-2026-0319 (QuickBooks DocNumber INV-2026-002), and request that finance correct it so the $500 appears on the client-facing bill. This is the write action for the prompt's ask to flag the credit issue to Marcus so finance can get it applied to the right invoice.
OE 12: Update StormCloud's CRM account notes using crm_create_engagement with engagement_type NOTE, company_ids including company_stormcloud, and a body summarizing the full account audit findings: the credit posting error (EST-2026-0287 vs INV-2026-0319), Jae-won Kim's housing situation (lease unsigned, guarantor packet not sent, Airtable and Linear prematurely closed, Omar's misprioritization), Liam Park's April 24 escalation covering four issues, Simone Richter's $340 stipend frozen under the blanket hold, and the Ridgeline Robotics referral status.
OE 13: Update the Airtable Client Accounts record for StormCloud using airtable_update_records with base_id appMoveOpsOps001, table_id tblClientAccts01, updating record recAcct000000063 with revised Notes reflecting the account audit findings and current risk status.
OE 14: Draft a check-in email to Liam Park using send_email with sender mina.hashimoto@moveops.com. The email should be addressed to liam.park@stormcloud.io and cover the credit correction (acknowledging it was not applied, explaining finance is fixing it now), an honest status update on Jae-won Kim's relocation (acknowledging the housing gap and what is being done), and the status of the other StormCloud relocations. Per the prompt instruction, this email should not be sent to Liam the agent should either send it to Mina's own address as a draft for review, or use move_email to relocate it from SENT to DRAFT after sending.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

OE 3: Search emails for StormCloud-related correspondence using search_emails with query StormCloud. OE 4: Search Slack for internal StormCloud discussions using conversations_search_messages with search_query StormCloud. OE 5: Search Linear for StormCloud-related issues using linear_list_issues with query StormCloud and includeArchived true. OE 10: Search CRM engagements for StormCloud using crm_list_engagements with company_ids including company_stormcloud to find existing engagement history and any prior notes about the account status, escalations, or recovery work.
OE 11: Send an email (via send_email) from mina.hashimoto@moveops.com to marcus.thorne@moveops.com flagging the $500 goodwill credit posting error. The email should explain that the credit was applied to cancelled estimate EST-2026-0287 instead of the active StormCloud invoice INV-2026-0319 (QuickBooks DocNumber INV-2026-002), and request that finance correct it so the $500 appears on the client-facing bill. This is the write action for the prompt's ask to flag the credit issue to Marcus so finance can get it applied to the right invoice.
OE 12: Update StormCloud's CRM account notes using crm_create_engagement with engagement_type NOTE, company_ids including company_stormcloud, and a body summarizing the full account audit findings: the credit posting error (EST-2026-0287 vs INV-2026-0319), Jae-won Kim's housing situation (lease unsigned, guarantor packet not sent, Airtable and Linear prematurely closed, Omar's misprioritization), Liam Park's April 24 escalation covering four issues, Simone Richter's $340 stipend frozen under the blanket hold, and the Ridgeline Robotics referral status.
OE 13: Update the Airtable Client Accounts record for StormCloud using airtable_update_records with base_id appMoveOpsOps001, table_id tblClientAccts01, updating record recAcct000000063 with revised Notes reflecting the account audit findings and current risk status.
OE 14: Draft a check-in email to Liam Park using send_email with sender mina.hashimoto@moveops.com. The email should be addressed to liam.park@stormcloud.io and cover the credit correction (acknowledging it was not applied, explaining finance is fixing it now), an honest status update on Jae-won Kim's relocation (acknowledging the housing gap and what is being done), and the status of the other StormCloud relocations. 1 An email was sent (via send_email) from [mina.hashimoto@moveops.com](mailto:mina.hashimoto@moveops.com) to [marcus.thorne@moveops.com](mailto:marcus.thorne@moveops.com) flagging the StormCloud Analytics $500 goodwill credit issue.
outcome

✓Run #2The trajectory shows a send_email call with sender 'mina.hashimoto@moveops.com' and recipients ['marcus.thorne@moveops.com'], with subject 'StormCloud $500 credit — need confirmation it's been moved to INV-2026-0319'. This clearly flags the StormCloud credit issue to Marcus.
✓Run #3The trajectory shows a send_email tool call with sender 'mina.hashimoto@moveops.com' and recipients ['marcus.thorne@moveops.com'], with subject 'ACTION NEEDED: StormCloud $500 goodwill credit still not applied to correct invoice'. This satisfies the criterion.
✓Run #4The trajectory shows a send_email tool call with sender 'mina.hashimoto@moveops.com' and recipients ['marcus.thorne@moveops.com'], subject 'Action needed: $500 goodwill credit still not on StormCloud invoice INV-2026-002'. This clearly passes.
✓Run #5The trajectory shows a send_email call with sender 'mina.hashimoto@moveops.com', recipients ['marcus.thorne@moveops.com'], and subject 'StormCloud $500 credit — need confirmation it's been moved to INV-2026-0319'. This clearly satisfies the criterion.
✓Run #6The trajectory shows a send_email tool call with sender 'mina.hashimoto@moveops.com' and recipients ['marcus.thorne@moveops.com'], subject referencing the StormCloud $500 goodwill credit issue. PASS.
2 The email body sent to [marcus.thorne@moveops.com](mailto:marcus.thorne@moveops.com) mentions that the $500 goodwill credit was applied to the wrong record specifically a cancelled estimate (EST-2026-0287 or similar reference) instead of the active StormCloud invoice (INV-2026-002 or INV-2026-0319 or similar).
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The email body sent to Marcus explicitly states the credit 'was posted against cancelled estimate EST-2026-0287 instead of the live invoice' and asks Marcus to 'Move the $500 credit from EST-2026-0287 to INV-2026-002'.
✓Run #2The email body sent to Marcus explicitly states: 'You confirmed the $500 was posted against cancelled estimate EST-2026-0287 instead of the active invoice INV-2026-0319.' Both the wrong record (EST-2026-0287) and the correct invoice (INV-2026-0319) are named.
✓Run #3The email to Marcus explicitly states: 'the credit was posted against cancelled estimate EST-2026-0287 instead of the live invoice' and 'The only StormCloud invoice I see in QuickBooks is INV-2026-002 ($26,400 total, $26,400 balance — no credit applied).' Both the cancelled estimate reference and the active invoice number are present.
✓Run #4The email body to Marcus explicitly states 'you confirmed the credit was misapplied to cancelled estimate EST-2026-0287 instead of the live StormCloud invoice' and references both INV-2026-002 and INV-2026-0319 as active invoice candidates.
✓Run #5The email body to Marcus explicitly states: 'You confirmed the $500 goodwill credit was misapplied to cancelled estimate EST-2026-0287 instead of the live invoice (INV-2026-0319 / INV-2026-002).' Both the cancelled estimate reference and the active invoice references are present.
✓Run #6The email body to Marcus explicitly states the credit was 'applied to cancelled estimate EST-2026-0287 instead of the active invoice INV-2026-0319.' Both document identifiers are present in the email body. PASS.

4 The CRM engagement note body or the final response specifically names Carmen Reyes at UrbanNest as the vendor contact who flagged the guarantor paperwork issue for Jae-won Kim's Austin lease on or around April 8.
outcome
 ✓✗✓✗✓✗ 50%
✓Run #1The CRM engagement note body explicitly states: 'Carmen Reyes flagged the blocker on 4/8' in connection with Jae-won Kim's lease guarantor paperwork. The final response also mentions 'Carmen flagged it 4/8'.
✗Run #2Neither the CRM engagement note body nor the final response mentions Carmen Reyes by name. The CRM note references 'UrbanNest flagged a missing corporate guarantor packet on April 8' but only names Suki Patel as the internal coordinator — the UrbanNest vendor contact Carmen Reyes is never identified.
✓Run #3The CRM engagement note body explicitly states: 'Suki Patel did not send the required corporate guarantor packet to UrbanNest after Carmen Reyes flagged it on 4/8.' Carmen Reyes is named as the UrbanNest contact who flagged the guarantor paperwork issue.
✗Run #4Neither the CRM engagement note body nor the final response mentions Carmen Reyes by name. The note references 'UrbanNest/Barton Creek Flats flagged on 4/8' generically, and the final response says 'UrbanNest flagged missing guarantor packet 4/8' — but no individual contact name is identified.
✓Run #5The CRM engagement note body explicitly states 'Corporate guarantor packet was to be sent to Carmen Reyes at UrbanNest on 4/24' and 'UrbanNest flagged on 4/8'. The final response also names 'Carmen Reyes' 4/8 request'. Carmen Reyes is clearly identified as the UrbanNest contact.
✗Run #6The Linear issue data retrieved by the agent explicitly names Carmen Reyes, but neither the CRM engagement note body nor the final response mentions Carmen Reyes by name. The CRM note says 'UrbanNest flagged...' without naming the contact. FAIL.
5 The CRM engagement note body or the final response specifically identifies Suki Patel as the MoveOps coordinator who acknowledged the guarantor paperwork need for Jae-won Kim's Austin lease but did not follow through with sending the documents to UrbanNest.
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The CRM engagement note body explicitly states: 'Suki Patel acknowledged the miss' in connection with Jae-won Kim's guarantor packet. The final response also states 'Suki dropped the guarantor packet after Carmen flagged it 4/8'.
